title: Slow-cooker pot roast beef
subtitle: Braising steak cooked all day until tender and saucy.
excerpt: A joint of beef chuck browned and cooked all day in the slow cooker with vegetables, stock and Worcestershire sauce until it yields completely and the cooking juices become a natural gravy. Serves six.
sourceNotes: Pot roast beef. Pot roasting (browning a joint and braising it in a covered pot with minimal liquid) is documented across British, French and American cooking. The method solves the problem of tough, collagen-rich cuts by converting that collagen to gelatin over many hours. The slow cooker is a modern version of the traditional lidded cast-iron pot left over a low fire.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice or shred the beef and freeze in the cooking liquid.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Pot roast reheats very well. Slice cold and reheat in the sauce.
recipe.temperatureNote: Slow cooker on LOW for 8 to 9 hours.

content.text: Beef chuck (sold as braising steak in British supermarkets) is the right cut: well-marbled with connective tissue that converts to gelatin during the long cook. Leaner cuts like topside dry out. The joint should be patted completely dry before browning so it sears rather than steams.

items.prepNote: beef, dissolved in 400 ml boiling water

content.text: Season {{beef-chuck}} of beef all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Heat {{rapeseed-oil}} of oil in a large, heavy frying pan over a high heat. Brown the joint on all sides, 3 to 4 minutes per side, until a deep brown crust forms. Transfer to the slow cooker.
Build the base and slow cook

In the same pan, cook {{onion}} of onion, {{carrot}} of carrot and {{celery}} of celery over a medium heat for 5 minutes. Add {{garlic}} of garlic and {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée and cook for 1 minute. Tip the vegetables into the slow cooker and nestle them around the beef. Pour over the dissolved {{stock-cube}} of stock and {{worcestershire-sauce}} of Worcestershire sauce. Tuck in {{thyme}} of thyme sprigs and {{bay-leaves}} of bay leaves. Cook on LOW for 8 to 9 hours until the beef is completely tender and starting to yield. Rest for 10 minutes before slicing or pulling apart. Remove the herbs and serve the cooking juices as a sauce.

items.symptom: Beef dry and stringy rather than yielding
items.cause: Wrong cut, or not cooked long enough.
items.fix: Only use chuck or braising steak. Topside and silverside do not have enough fat and connective tissue. Add another hour if the meat is not yet tender.

items.cause: Too much liquid, or a lean cut with little collagen.
items.fix: Lift the beef out and reduce the liquid in a saucepan over high heat for 10 minutes. If the sauce still lacks body, stir in a teaspoon of cornflour mixed with cold water.

items.cause: Joint too large for the slow cooker, or cooking on HIGH rather than LOW.
items.fix: A 1.5 kg joint should fit most 6 L slow cookers. Low and slow is essential: the HIGH setting is faster but can dry out the outside before the centre is cooked.

content.text: Pot roast beef is a Sunday dinner that requires almost no active cooking time. Set it up before you leave the house and come back to something ready to serve. Serve with roast potatoes or mashed potato and the cooking juices as a thin, flavourful gravy. The beef shreds or slices easily and leftovers reheat well.
